canonical_definition: The tendency of a model to exhibit unconscious, automatic attitudes or stereotypes that influence its decisions and behaviors without being explicitly directed to do so.
papers: Reliable Evaluation and Benchmarks for Statement Autoformalization

relationships.evidence: We propose VISBIAS, which detects both explicit biases (multiple-choice/yes-no questions) and implicit biases (image description/form-completion tasks).
